understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by moving the selectable piece(s) so that every goal tile marked with a '+' in the semantic grid was occupied by a selectable simultaneously; once all levels report WIN, the whole game is finished within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT) shift the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in that direction, unless blocked or interacting with special boundaries; some adjacent objects can be pushed if they are pushable and directly in the movement direction, causing them to move along with the selected piece. Walls/boundaries can block movement (sometimes with non-uniform behavior, so empirical testing matters). If multiple selectables exist, control switches via CLICK on the other piece’s exact pixel/grid position as listed in the state, and the movement.*_blocked flags are only heuristics indicating adjacency and do not guarantee success or failure.

history.summary: Level 1 attempt 2/2 failed after 64 turns. No attempts remaining.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by moving the selectable piece(s) so that every goal tile marked with a '+' in the semantic grid was occupied by a selectable simultaneously; once all levels report WIN, the whole game is finished within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT) shift the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in that direction, unless blocked or interacting with special boundaries; some adjacent objects can be pushed if they are pushable and directly in the movement direction, causing them to move along with the selected piece. Walls/boundaries can block movement (sometimes with non-uniform behavior, so empirical testing matters). If multiple selectables exist, control switches via CLICK on the other piece’s exact pixel/grid position as listed in the state, and the movement.*_blocked flags are only heuristics indicating adjacency and do not guarantee success or failure.
